name: Amaretto Hazelnut Macaroon Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Hazelnuts - roast for 10 minutes at 350°F
recipeInstructions:
Hazelnut macaroon crust:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 10-inch springform pan.
Line the bottom with parchment paper (do not use wax paper as it sticks horribly).
Grease the parchment.
Whisk together egg whites and vanilla extract until soft peaks form.
Remove as much skin from hazelnuts as convenient, chop nuts in food processor with 1 cup powdered sugar for 30 seconds.
Add both powdered and granulated sugars, pulse a few times to combine.
With processor running, pour in egg mixture. Process for 15 seconds until smooth.
Reserve half of the batter. Pour remaining into springform pan, smooth with spatula.
Pour reserved batter onto greased parchment on cookie sheet, spread into a 7-8 inch disk.
Bake crust for 25-30 minutes (disk for 20-25 minutes). Cool on wire rack.
Chop off the cooled disk into 1/8-inch pieces and soak in 1/4 cup Amaretto liqueur. Do not soak for more than 15 minutes or they get soggy.
Remove crust carefully. Replace bottom of springform pan with foil-wrapped cardboard circle.
Amaretto cheesecake filling:
Sprinkle gelatin over 1/4 cup Amaretto, let stand 5 minutes.
Heat mixture in saucepan with hot (not boiling) water, stirring for 4 minutes.
Leave in hot water to stay warm.
Beat cream cheese in mixer until smooth. Add lemon juice and zest, mix.
Beat cream to soft peaks. Fold 1/3 of the cream into the cream cheese. Fold in remaining whipped cream.
Fold in soaked macaroon disk bits.
Scrape filling into prepared pan. Cover with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate for at least 3 hours (preferably overnight).
